reasons: Lolita directly reduces Chang'e's long-range projectile pressure by blocking the path of her main poke and Meteor Shower damage.
She protects grouped teammates from Chang'e's front-facing damage window, forcing Chang'e to reposition or delay her ultimate.

proof.summary: Chang'e wants to pressure from range with projectile-heavy damage, while Lolita can raise Guardian's Bulwark to block projectiles and reduce the value of Chang'e's main damage window.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces the correct direction before Chang'e commits Meteor Shower.
The team groups behind Lolita instead of scattering into Chang'e's line.
failureCases: Chang'e changes angle and fires around the shield.
Lolita wastes her shield before Chang'e commits ultimate.
Chang'e's team forces Lolita to turn away from the projectile path.

reasons: Natalia can punish Chang'e before Chang'e gets a safe long-range setup.
Chang'e is easier to kill when Natalia reaches her from flank or bush before Chang'e has time to kite backward.

proof.summary: Chang'e wants to stand safely behind her frontline, while Natalia pressures hidden angles and punishes Chang'e when her map awareness or protection is weak.
worksBestWhen: Natalia approaches from fog, side bushes, or after Chang'e uses mobility or shield tools.
Natalia targets Chang'e before Meteor Shower creates distance pressure.
failureCases: Natalia crosses visible traps, minions, or vision.
Chang'e keeps enough distance to kite before Natalia commits.

reasons: Hayabusa can reach Chang'e through flank angles and punish her low durability when she is separated.
Shadow mobility lets Hayabusa avoid some linear poke patterns and threaten Chang'e after her main damage is committed.

proof.summary: Chang'e wants to keep distance and fire forward, while Hayabusa can enter from shadow angles and burst isolated backline targets.
worksBestWhen: Chang'e is away from teammates or minions.
Hayabusa saves mobility to enter and exit after forcing Chang'e's defensive tools.
failureCases: Chang'e stays grouped and Hayabusa's ultimate damage spreads.
Hayabusa dives into hard crowd control.
Chang'e's team tracks Hayabusa's shadow positions.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge through projectile pressure and threaten Chang'e's backline position.
His mobility and invulnerable timing reduce Chang'e's ability to punish him during his engage.

proof.summary: Chang'e wants to zone with forward damage, while Lancelot can use dash and invulnerability windows to bypass poke and burst her during cooldown gaps.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits for Chang'e to commit Meteor Shower or misposition.
Lancelot has minions or targets to extend his dash path.
failureCases: Lancelot enters without dash reset options.
Chang'e is protected by reliable crowd control.
Chang'e's shield and movement speed let her kite out before Lancelot finishes.

reasons: Franco can punish Chang'e's predictable firing position with hook or suppression.
Chang'e loses much of her value when she is forced out of safe backline spacing.

proof.summary: Chang'e wants uninterrupted range control, while Franco can hook or suppress her when she steps forward to aim Meteor Shower.
worksBestWhen: Franco hides his angle before Chang'e starts channeling pressure.
Franco's team follows up immediately after hook or suppression.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and Chang'e keeps range advantage.
Chang'e plays behind minions or tank body-blocks.
Franco suppresses without enough burst follow-up.
